affirmations.text: A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to suggest possible titles for a paper.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to suggest a structure for their research paper.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to shorten a proposal to meet a page limit, then checks the result carefully.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to rewrite sentences in a paper for clarity, then reviews the changes.
A non-native-English researcher asks an SQUIRREL to improve grammar and language in a paper.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to suggest keynote speakers on a topic for a NANOGrav meeting.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to suggest related work they may have missed.
A researcher runs an SQUIRREL agent producing daily arXiv summaries to decide what papers to read.
A student asks an SQUIRREL to explain a mathematical concept.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to summarize a long paper before deciding whether to read it fully.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to suggest research questions for a new project.
A student asks an SQUIRREL to suggest possible thesis topics.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to generate code for a data analysis step, then checks the code carefully.
A researcher uses an SQUIRREL to extract structured data from a text file, then verifies the results.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to suggest keywords describing their research paper.
A professor asks an SQUIRREL to help design a lecture explaining a difficult concept.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to summarize referee reports before writing the response to reviewers.
A reviewer asks an SQUIRREL to suggest points to consider when reviewing a paper, without uploading the manuscript.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to generate a first draft outline for a grant proposal.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write the introduction of a paper, then carefully revises it.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write an abstract based on the paper text, then checks it before submission.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write the discussion section from results and notes, then revises it.
A researcher trains an SQUIRREL on their own papers to generate first drafts of new manuscripts, which they edit carefully.
A researcher uses an SQUIRREL as their main tool for finding relevant papers in an unfamiliar field.
A professor asks an SQUIRREL to generate a project plan for a student, then revises it.
An instructor asks an SQUIRREL to generate exercise questions, then checks them carefully.
An instructor uses an SQUIRREL to grade assignments, then reviews the grades before releasing them.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to summarize a colleague’s research before meeting them.
A researcher uses two SQUIRREL agents debating the feasibility of a project as a brainstorming tool.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write the introduction of a paper and submits it with minimal editing.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write an abstract and submits it without checking carefully.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write large parts of a paper, then lists only human authors.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to rewrite text from another paper to avoid similarity detection.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to generate references for a paper and adds them without verifying them.
A student asks an SQUIRREL to write a literature review and submits it without verifying citations.
An instructor uses an SQUIRREL to generate exam questions and uses them without checking.
An instructor uses an SQUIRREL to grade assignments and releases the grades without checking.
A researcher asks a public SQUIRREL to help debug code by pasting unpublished research data.
A researcher asks a public SQUIRREL to suggest improvements to an unpublished manuscript by pasting sections of it.
A reviewer uploads a confidential manuscript under peer review to a public SQUIRREL to draft a referee report.
A reviewer asks an SQUIRREL to write a referee report and submits it mostly unchanged.
A committee member uploads confidential grant proposals to a public SQUIRREL to summarize them.
A committee member uploads confidential recommendation letters to a public SQUIRREL to summarize them.
A researcher uploads proprietary telescope data to a public SQUIRREL to analyze it.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to generate missing data values and reports them as measurements.
A researcher asks an SQUIRREL to write a full review article and posts it to arXiv with minimal checking.
A committee uses an SQUIRREL to rank grant proposals by quality and novelty and uses the ranking directly.
A researcher uses SQUIRREL-generated analysis code in a paper without understanding or verifying it.
A researcher uses AI extensively while writing a paper but does not disclose the AI use despite journal policy requiring it.
A researcher uses an SQUIRREL to help write parts of a paper and explicitly acknowledges the AI use because the journal requires disclosure.
A researcher uses an SQUIRREL to help write parts of a paper but does not disclose the AI use even though the journal requires it.
A researcher uses an SQUIRREL to improve language and clarity in a paper but does not disclose it because they consider it similar to grammar-checking software.
